pi_items.source: export_result.sh and verifier.py confirm this output path
Wikipedia confirms: Vaduz (capital, along Rhine), Balzers (situated along east bank of Rhine), Schaan (nestled near Rhine River). All three exist in Geofabrik OSM shapefile.

Wikipedia confirms: Malbun at 1,600m elevation in Alps (highest village in Liechtenstein), Steg at 1,295m+ elevation in Triesenberg mountains. Both are in Upper Country (Oberland), far from Rhine Valley. Both exist in Geofabrik OSM shapefile.
Task description specifies '1 kilometer (1000 meters) buffer'. Geofabrik data confirms Rhine as western border. Towns' proximity verified through coordinates and geographic sources.
setup_task.sh line 34 defines DATA_URL. Geofabrik page confirms shapefiles contain gis_osm_waterways_free_1.shp and gis_osm_places_free_1.shp
Wikipedia confirms Liechtenstein geography. Rhine forms western border with Switzerland.

privileged_info_summary: The flood risk assessment task uses verified real OpenStreetMap data from Geofabrik covering Liechtenstein. The metadata correctly identifies geographic features: Vaduz (capital on Rhine), Balzers (along east bank of Rhine), and Schaan (near Rhine Valley) as towns at risk within a 1km flood buffer. Malbun (at 1,600m elevation in the Alps) and Steg (at 1,295m+ elevation in Triesenberg mountains) are correctly identified as safe towns outside the flood zone. The 1000-meter buffer distance is explicitly stated in the task description. All towns and geographic claims have been verified against current Wikipedia sources and the actual Geofabrik OpenStreetMap shapefile data.
